concepts.lesson: Prime factorization is the process of breaking down a composite number into its prime factors. Every number greater than 1 has a unique prime factorization, which cannot be broken down further into smaller prime numbers. To find the prime factorization of a number, we need to identify all the prime numbers that multiply together to give the original number.
key_points: Every composite number can be broken down into its prime factors
Prime factorization is unique for each number
Prime factors cannot be broken down further

concepts.lesson: Two numbers are co-prime if they have no common prime factors. In other words, the greatest common divisor (GCD) of two co-prime numbers is 1. To determine if two numbers are co-prime, we need to find their prime factorizations and compare them.
key_points: Two numbers have no common prime factors
Greatest Common Divisor (GCD) of co-prime numbers is 1
Co-prime numbers cannot be divided by a common divisor

concepts.lesson: Divisibility tests are used to determine if one number can divide another. A divisibility test checks the remainder when the dividend is divided by the divisor. For example, the divisibility rule for 2 states that a number is divisible by 2 if its last digit is even.
key_points: Divisibility tests check the remainder of division
Remainder determines whether one number can divide another
Different divisibility rules apply to different numbers

concepts.lesson: The Greatest Common Divisor (GCD) of two or more numbers is the largest number that divides all of them without leaving a remainder. To find the GCD, we need to identify all common factors and choose the largest one.
key_points: GCD is the largest number dividing multiple numbers
Identify all common factors to find the GCD
GCD cannot be broken down further

concepts.lesson: A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself. Prime numbers play a crucial role in mathematics, particularly in cryptography and coding theory.
key_points: Prime numbers have only two divisors: 1 and themselves
Prime numbers are essential in mathematics, especially in cryptography
Every number greater than 1 can be broken down into prime factors

concepts.lesson: A composite number is a natural number greater than 1 that has at least one positive divisor other than 1 and itself. Composite numbers are the building blocks of arithmetic, as they can be broken down into prime factors.
key_points: Composite numbers have more than two divisors
Every composite number can be broken down into prime factors
Composite numbers are essential in arithmetic

concepts.lesson: Number theory is the branch of mathematics dealing with properties and behavior of integers, particularly divisibility, primality, and congruences. Number theory has numerous applications in cryptography, coding theory, and computer science.
key_points: Number theory focuses on integer properties
Divisibility, primality, and congruences are key concepts in number theory
Applications of number theory include cryptography and coding theory

concepts.lesson: A congruence modulo n is a statement that two numbers have the same remainder when divided by n. Congruences are used to solve equations in number theory and cryptography.
key_points: Congruence mod n states that two numbers leave the same remainder
Congruences are essential in solving number theory equations
Applications of congruences include cryptography

concepts.lesson: Cryptography is the practice and study of techniques for secure communication, particularly encryption, decryption, and authentication. Cryptography relies heavily on number theory and prime numbers.
key_points: Cryptography ensures secure communication
Encryption, decryption, and authentication are key concepts in cryptography
Number theory is essential in cryptography

concepts.lesson: Modular arithmetic is a system of arithmetic that deals with remainders when numbers are divided by n. Modular arithmetic has numerous applications in number theory, algebra, and computer science.
key_points: Modular arithmetic focuses on remainders of division
Applications of modular arithmetic include number theory and computer science
Modular arithmetic relies heavily on congruences

concepts.lesson: The Euclidean algorithm is a method for finding the greatest common divisor (GCD) of two numbers, based on repeated application of the division algorithm. The Euclidean algorithm has numerous applications in number theory and algebra.
key_points: Euclidean algorithm finds GCD using repeated division
Applications of the Euclidean algorithm include number theory and algebra
The Euclidean algorithm relies on modular arithmetic

concepts.lesson: Fermat's little theorem states that if p is a prime number, then for any integer a not divisible by p, the following congruence holds: a^(p-1) ≡ 1 (mod p). Fermat's little theorem has numerous applications in number theory and cryptography.
key_points: Fermat's Little Theorem relates prime numbers to congruences
Applications of Fermat's Little Theorem include number theory and cryptography
The theorem relies heavily on modular arithmetic

concepts.lesson: The Chinese remainder theorem states that if we have a system of simultaneous congruences with pairwise coprime moduli, then there is exactly one solution modulo the product of the moduli. The Chinese remainder theorem has numerous applications in number theory and cryptography.
key_points: Chinese Remainder Theorem solves systems of congruences
Applications of the theorem include number theory and cryptography
The theorem relies heavily on modular arithmetic
